site_tagline: Recurring patterns across languages, space, and time.
footer_text: Language Patterns — PoC. Cross-linguistic typology and diachrony. Seed data is illustrative; sources to be added.
footer_license_html: Content licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0. Attribute as “Language Patterns (languagepatterns.org)”.
home_title: Explore how languages share, differ, and change.
home_subtitle: Language Patterns documents recurring strategies, pathways, and cycles across the world's languages — from how unrelated languages express “there is” to how negation rebuilds itself over centuries.
section_geographic_distribution_hint: Each dot is one attested language, coloured by the strategy it uses. Click a dot for the surface form.
section_examples_hint: Toggle between Natural / Literal / Gloss to see how each language conceptualises the same idea.
p_graveyard_description: A sound-change pathway catalogue tracking the disappearance of /p/ across the world's languages.
